prose: Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene were computed at the GFN2-xTB level (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Mulliken partial charges were used. The molecule contains 21 atoms (10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe). The iron centre (atom 11) is both the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191), consistent with Fe d-orbital dominance in ferrocene. The dual descriptor for Fe is −0.030527354 (nucleophilic character). All integrity checks passed (f⁺ and f⁻ sum to 1.0000; trustworthy: true). No warnings were reported by the engine. The headline value reported is the f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui function) on Fe, the most electrophilic atom.
